basis: Synthetic hand-worked example (see BASIS.md, calc 0.4.0 section). NOT an FTA-certified figure; regression anchor only. Pins vrh_v0 CALC_VERSION 0.4.0 (trip-level excision, handoff 0004) over fixture_block_v04.json, with the retained 0.3.0/0.2.0 comparison values on the SAME positions, and the clean two-trip fixture_block.json under 0.4.0.

block_v04_fixture.comment: One vehicle (veh-401), one block (blk-4), three trips: F (clean), G (400 s within-trip gap), H (clean), 600 s layovers F-G and G-H. v0.4 keeps trips F+H's running time (300 s + 300 s), excises trip G and drops BOTH adjacent layover intervals (a layover interval counts only when both bounding trips are clean). Trip-denominated coverage 2/3 blocks at the default 0.95 threshold, so the passing case runs at an explicit 0.5.

vrh_v0_4_default_threshold_blocked.comment: At the default coverage_threshold 0.95: 2 clean trips < 0.95 x 3 -> blocked, value None, one blocking coverage_below_threshold finding citing the excised trip's records.

vrh_v0_3_comparison.comment: Retained compute_vrh_v0_3 over the identical positions at coverage_threshold 0 (block-denominated coverage would be 0/1): block-level exclusion drops the ENTIRE block -> 0.00 h. v0.4 (0.17 h) >= v0.3 (0.00 h): block exclusion is strictly harsher.

vrh_v0_2_comparison.comment: Retained compute_vrh_v0_2 over the identical positions at coverage_threshold 0.5: per-trip exclusion also drops only trip-G (0.17 h) but never counted any layover; on this fixture no clean-adjacent layover survives excision, so v0.4 == v0.2 == 0.17 h (v0.4 >= v0.2 in general).

clean_block_fixture_v0_4.comment: fixture_block.json (two CLEAN trips, 600 s layover) under v0.4: nothing is excised, the clean-adjacent layover interval stays included -> the 0.3.0 value 0.33 h is reproduced exactly, detail now trip-denominated.
